banner.title: CBSE EXAMS POSTPONED (MARCH 2)
banner.subtitle: Official Notice Inside — 7 Gulf Countries Affected

cbse.summary: CBSE has officially postponed Class 10 and Class 12 board exams scheduled for March 2, 2026 in 7 Middle East countries. New dates to be announced after review on March 3.
cbse.issuedBy: Controller of Examinations, Dr. Sanyam Bhardwaj

madad.description: Register your grievance or track existing cases related to Indians abroad

meaCentral.description: Ministry of External Affairs — 24/7 emergency helpline for Indians abroad

embassies.note: Embassy operating emergency-only; regular consular services closed until further notice.

airlines.name: Emirates
airlines.message: Emirates will operate a limited number of flights commencing the evening of 2 March; all other flights remain suspended until further notice. Do not go to the airport unless notified by Emirates.
actions: Check flight status and wait for airline confirmation before going to the airport.
If affected, rebook for travel on or before 20 March, or request a refund.

airlines.name: Qatar Airways
airlines.message: Qatar Airways operations remain temporarily suspended due to closure of Qatari airspace; next update March 3 by 09:00 Doha time.
actions: Monitor updates on qatarairways.com or the mobile app.
Contact your airline/travel agent for rebooking/refund guidance.

airlines.name: Etihad
airlines.message: All flights to and from Abu Dhabi are suspended until 14:00 UAE time on March 2; some repatriation/cargo flights may operate with UAE approvals.
actions: Check flight status on etihad.com before heading to the airport.
Eligible tickets may be rebooked free of charge; refunds available per airline policy.

airlines.name: FlyDubai
airlines.message: All operations to and from Dubai suspended until 15:00 UAE time March 2.
actions: Do not go to the airport unless confirmed by airline.

airlines.name: Air Arabia
airlines.message: Operations to/from UAE suspended until 15:00 UAE time March 2.
actions: Do not go to the airport unless confirmed by airline.

airlines.name: IndiGo
airlines.message: IndiGo has temporarily suspended flights to/from affected Gulf airports through March 2 (2359 IST).
actions: Use rebooking/refund options per airline advisory.

airlines.name: Air India Express
airlines.message: Air India Express has suspended services to Gulf destinations through March 2 (2359 IST).
actions: Use rebooking/refund options per airline advisory.

airlines.name: Air India
airlines.message: Air India has cancelled multiple Gulf and Europe/US flights and rerouted some services due to airspace disruptions.
actions: Verify your flight status before going to the airport.
Contact Air India for rebooking/refunds per advisory.

airlines.name: Akasa Air
airlines.message: Akasa Air suspended Abu Dhabi, Doha, Jeddah, Kuwait, Riyadh routes through March 2.
actions: Check akasaair.com and your email/SMS for updates.
Use rebooking/refund options per airline advisory.

airports.name: Dubai International Airport
airports.message: Dubai Airports says limited operations are expected to resume later today (March 2) with a small number of flights from DXB; do not go to the airport unless your airline confirms departure time.

airports.name: Dubai World Central (Al Maktoum) Airport
airports.message: Dubai Airports says limited operations are expected to resume later today (March 2) with a small number of flights from DWC; do not go to the airport unless your airline confirms departure time.

airports.name: Hamad International Airport (Doha)
airports.message: Operations severely disrupted due to Qatari airspace closure; expect cancellations and delays.

airports.name: Zayed International Airport (Abu Dhabi)
airports.message: Etihad has suspended scheduled flights to/from Abu Dhabi (AUH) until 14:00 UAE time March 2; check with your airline before heading to the airport.

airports.name: Sharjah International Airport
airports.message: Sharjah Airport operations suspended (per earlier official updates).

airports.name: Bahrain International Airport
airports.message: Expect disruption due to regional airspace situation; check airline for updates.

airports.name: Kuwait International Airport
airports.message: Expect disruption due to regional airspace situation; check airline for updates.

advisories.title: DXB/DWC: Limited operations expected to resume March 2 (evening)
advisories.summary: Dubai Airports says a small number of flights will operate from DXB and DWC later today (March 2); passengers should not go to the airport unless their airline confirms departure time.

advisories.title: Emirates: Limited flights from evening March 2; others suspended until further notice
advisories.summary: Emirates will operate a limited number of flights from the evening of March 2; all other flights remain suspended until further notice and passengers should wait for airline confirmation before going to the airport.

advisories.title: Qatar Airways: Operations suspended; next update by Mar 3 09:00 Doha
advisories.summary: Qatar Airways says its operations remain suspended due to Qatari airspace closure and it will issue another update by March 3 09:00 Doha time.

advisories.title: CBSE Board Exams (Middle East): March 2 papers postponed
advisories.summary: CBSE postponed Class 10 and 12 exams scheduled for March 2 in 7 Gulf/Middle East countries; new dates will be announced after review on March 3.

resources.name: Kerala NORKA Helpdesk
resources.details: Helpdesk for Gulf expatriates; contact via NORKA roots (official channels).

safety.title: Airport incidents reported
safety.summary: Reports indicate casualties and damage at some Gulf airports during the missile/drone attacks; follow local authority updates and avoid travel without airline confirmation.

notes: Always verify flight status with your airline before travelling to the airport.
If you are stranded, contact the nearest Indian Embassy/Consulate helpline above.
disclaimer: This hub aggregates official advisories and credible reporting for Indian nationals. Always follow local authorities and your airline/embassy guidance.
